category_title: Students with Intellectual Disability (ID) receiving individualized support in inclusive settings
student_description: This student requires a delicate balance between individualized academic support and social integration. They easily fall off-task without adapted curriculum and direct instructions, but become frustrated and socially isolated if given excessive 1-1 support when capable of independent work. They uniquely need a support system that provides targeted help to initiate tasks while actively promoting peer collaboration and sustained interaction with general education teachers.

criteria.rationale: Sustained 1-1 support isolates this student socially; they need structured opportunities to interact with peers.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes specific strategies to transition the student from 1-1 support into cooperative learning groups or peer-assisted activities.

criteria.rationale: The student thrives when the general education teacher maintains an active role in their instruction and behavior management.
criteria.check: The long-term plan explicitly outlines how the general education teacher will remain actively involved in the student's instruction and behavior management, rather than delegating solely to a specialized tutor.
